text: The exercise involves grasping and picking up a blue ring located on the right side of a ring tower using robotic instruments.
The participant maneuvers the blue ring around the wire structure, ensuring no contact is made between the ring and the wire.
The participant grabs the blue ring in a supinated position, using wrist articulation and range of motion to navigate around the wire structure without making contact.

The participant removes the blue ring from the wire structure using wrist articulation, then transfers it to the other hand and navigates towards the left side of the screen to place the ring at the base of another wire structure.
The participant removes the blue ring from the top of the dome structure, holding it in a supinated position as part of the exercise.
The participant approaches the wire structure in a pronated position to place the blue ring back around it, demonstrating careful navigation to avoid contact with the wire.
The left hand grabs the second ring at the top of the dome using a supinated position, preparing to navigate the wire structure without contact.
The surgeon carefully maneuvers the ring around the wire structure, minimizing contact and force to prevent dislodging the loosely attached wire from the surface.
The surgeon navigates towards screen right, holding the ring in a pronated position to optimize range of motion while placing it back onto the wire structure.
The surgeon carefully navigates through the wire course, moving slowly and deliberately to avoid penalties, while handling the ring with precision.
